premise: When my husband died, his $290,000 Bordeaux wine collection was supposed to pass to me, but the adult stepdaughter I’d raised since she was ten produced a fake notarized gift letter, cleared the cellar, and sold every bottle at auction for cash. I proved the notary stamp was fabricated, won a $310,000 civil fraud judgment, and when she refused to pay I garnished her six-figure trust fund from her maternal grandmother until the debt was satisfied—then donated the final $20,000 to an anti-forgery education program in her name.
